description:
You are an MLOps engineer who has just finished an experiment run.  
    All raw artifacts have already been placed for you in:  

        /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/

    Inside that directory you will find exactly four files:

        accuracy.txt      – a one–line text file
        loss.txt          – a one–line text file
        params.json       – a small JSON file (pretty printed, multiple lines)
        model.bin         – a binary model snapshot (≈ 1 KB, already present)

    Your job is to prepare the “production hand-off” bundle that the data-science
    team expects.  They need:

    1.  A **compressed archive** that contains only the human–readable artifacts
        (accuracy.txt, loss.txt and params.json).  
        •  Name the archive:
               run_2024_03_18_artifacts.tar.gz  
        •  Place it in the directory:
               /home/user/experiments/archives/  
           (create the directory if it does not yet exist).  

    2.  A **checksum manifest** describing every file inside that archive.  
        •  Create the file
               /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/artifact_checksums.log  
        •  For each file that went into the archive (exactly three files, in the
           alphabetical order shown below) write one line to the manifest using
           this exact CSV schema:

               <filename>,<sha256_checksum>

           The final required order and format therefore looks like:

               accuracy.txt,<64-hex-digit-sha256>
               loss.txt,<64-hex-digit-sha256>
               params.json,<64-hex-digit-sha256>

           Notes about the format that **will be automatically verified**:
             •  Lower-case hex only, no spaces.
             •  Exactly one comma per line.
             •  Each line must end with a single trailing newline character
                (i.e. the file’s final byte must be ‘\n’).
             •  No extra header, no extra blank lines, no trailing whitespace.  

    3.  A quick confirmation message printed to STDOUT informing the user that the
        bundle and manifest were created successfully.  (The text itself is not
        strictly verified, but human reviewers will see it; keep it short.)

    You are free to use any standard Linux command-line utilities that are
    available in a typical distribution (tar, sha256sum, printf/echo, mkdir
    … etc.).  No elevated privileges are required.  The entire task can be
    completed with roughly three or four shell commands.

    When you are done, you should be able to:

       •  `tar -tzf /home/user/experiments/archives/run_2024_03_18_artifacts.tar.gz`
          and see only:
              accuracy.txt
              loss.txt
              params.json

       •  `sha256sum -c artifact_checksums.log` (executed inside
          /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/) returns “OK” for all three
          lines (the automated tests will perform the equivalent check).

    Important:  do NOT include model.bin in either the archive or in the log.
truth:
<!--  Files that already exist before the agent starts  -->
    /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/accuracy.txt  (content: "accuracy:0.9125\n")
    /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/loss.txt      (content: "loss:0.0873\n")
    /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/params.json   (content:
    "{\n  \"learning_rate\": 0.001,\n  \"batch_size\": 32,\n  \"epochs\": 10\n}\n")
    /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/model.bin     (binary, size ≈ 1 KB)

    --------------------------------------------------------------------------
    Expected artefacts AFTER the agent finishes
    --------------------------------------------------------------------------

    1.  Archive
        Path: /home/user/experiments/archives/run_2024_03_18_artifacts.tar.gz
        Must contain exactly three files whose *internal* paths are:
            accuracy.txt
            loss.txt
            params.json
        (The test suite will expand and verify both membership and byte-level
         equality with the source files above.)

    2.  Manifest
        Path: /home/user/experiments/run_2024_03_18/artifact_checksums.log
        Content: three lines, in exactly this order:
            accuracy.txt,<SHA256_of_accuracy.txt>\n
            loss.txt,<SHA256_of_loss.txt>\n
            params.json,<SHA256_of_params.json>\n
        The `<SHA256_of_…>` placeholders represent the real checksums that are
        computed from the source files above.  During testing the harness will
        compute the hashes itself and compare them with the strings provided by
        the agent, so the literal hash values are not hard-coded here.

    3.  No additional files are expected; model.bin must remain untouched and
        must not appear in either the archive or the manifest.

    4.  Directory /home/user/experiments/archives/ must exist (it may be newly
        created by the agent) and be user-writable (mode 755 or 775 is fine).

    The test rig will:
      •  verify the presence and contents of the archive & manifest,
      •  confirm line formatting, ordering and checksums,
      •  ensure model.bin is excluded from both derived artefacts.
